title: Status indicators must use color meaningfully and accessibly
summary: AI-generated interfaces use colored badges and status dots without consistent semantic mapping, leaving color as the only differentiator without text labels.
instruction: Every status indicator must combine color with a text label, icon, or other non-color differentiator. Color must have a documented semantic mapping (red=error, green=success, yellow=warning, gray=inactive). Do not use color as the sole differentiator.
rationale: Approximately 8% of users have some form of color vision deficiency. Relying solely on color for status indication excludes these users. Additionally, color-only indicators fail when viewed on grayscale screens, in high-contrast mode, or when printed.
violations: A deployment dashboard showing status as colored dots (red, green, yellow) without text labels, icons, or any non-color identifier.
A user table with a 'Status' column showing only green or red circles, no text, no pattern, no position coding.
controls: A deployment dashboard with colored dots AND text labels (Active, Error, Pending) AND icon indicators (checkmark, warning, clock).
A user table with a Status column using colored badges that include text ('Active', 'Suspended', 'Pending') plus subtle icons.
borderlineCases: Real-time status indicators on hardware devices (cameras, sensors) where space is extremely limited and text labels are impractical - but should still have tooltip text.
contextualExceptions: Gaming platform status indicators where color convention is widely established and health/ammo bars use position as secondary coding.
Physical-device UI emulators where space constraints require minimal indicators.
detectorSignal: Colored elements (badge, dot, circle) with no adjacent text label, icon, or aria-label describing their meaning
matcherProposal: Find small colored elements (width/height under 24px, circular or rounded) that are positioned as status indicators. Check for nearby text within the same container. Check for aria-label on the element. Flag if color is the sole differentiator.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine semantic color mapping from CSS alone.
May flag decorative colored elements (brand accent dots) that are not status indicators.
Cannot distinguish properly implemented color+pattern indicators from text-based alternatives.
falsePositiveScenarios: A brand's decorative accent elements (colored dots used as ornamentation) that are not status indicators.
A step indicator where numbered steps already provide non-color differentiation.
A well-known traffic-light convention (red/yellow/green) where position provides secondary coding.
mitigationStrategy: Limit to elements within status/badge/indicator contexts. Flag only when multiple color-only indicators exist. Exclude decorative elements.

fixturePlans.violation: A user management page with Status column showing only colored circles: red, green, yellow, gray with no text.
fixturePlans.control: Same page with colored circles AND text labels: 'Active (green)', 'Suspended (red)', 'Pending (yellow)', 'Inactive (gray)'.
fixturePlans.borderline: A server rack dashboard with colored dots (24x24px) without text labels but with position legend at the top of the page.

cleanRoomAttestations: WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Standard accessibility requirement.
overlapNotes: Distinct from existing rules. No badges/indicators rule exists in the catalog.
